event.summary: Claimed one ready iteration unit without expanding repository or path scope.

acceptance.proof: The composition consumes only accepted N and P trees, validates fixed root/shape invariants, checks total and target bounds before one exact reserve, remaps description parents without recursion, and delegates final structural limits.

All seven accepted cases execute through the local components with six static leaves, eleven volatile leaves, and desc in exact accepted LSLC-001H order while preserving represented values and nested description order.
All source component names and character data move without cloning; only description parent indexes receive the fixed eleven-node offset and both component arenas remain represented exactly once.

The result remains a compact local element tree and adds no declaration, observed whitespace, self-closing policy, endpoint bytes, parser, provider, clock/host/identity generation, networking, runtime, device, feature, or Manifold authority.
Cargo files, dependencies, feature lock, effect union, accepted evidence, oracle drivers, implementation-source boundary, devices, and runtime surfaces remain unchanged and inert.
Public instructions distinguish local ordered element composition from document spelling, providers, runtime, and authority; shared skills are reviewed and workflow remains device-free.

document.commit_policy: Commit the ordered accepted-element merge separately from XML declarations, observed whitespace/self-closing spelling, complete document bytes, parsers, providers, protocol, transport, adapters, runtime activation, features, devices, or external integration.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route exact N/P merge order, parent remapping, allocation preservation, and document/provider/runtime boundary.
Describe the ordered local element tree and remaining document-spelling/provider work without overclaim.
Review whether accepted-component ordering adds reusable guidance; retain unchanged if current composition and artifact-role rules cover it.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review the installed skill and run its instruction-surface whitespace check.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Review whether this source-only Rusty LSL composition changes repo-family routing; retain unchanged if current boundaries cover it.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review the installed skill and run its instruction-surface whitespace check.

non_scope: Any change to Cargo.toml, Cargo.lock, project or feature locks, accepted fixtures, oracle drivers, dependencies, Cargo features, unsafe Rust, generated protocol material, native libraries, or implementation-source inputs.
XML declaration, inter-element whitespace, self-closing tags, attributes, namespaces, schemas, parsing, decoding, canonicalization, endpoint bytes, or complete-document compatibility.

Provider traits or selection, acquisition, clocks, host/environment inspection, identity generation, address/port semantics, sockets, networking, discovery, inlet, outlet, buffering, recovery, FFI, adapters, or runtime activation.
Protocol, wire, transport, ecosystem compatibility, device, Quest, Hostess, platform lifecycle, product policy, or external integration claims.
Manifold admission, registry, subscription, route, lease, revision, replay, identity, authorization, audit, command, session, topology, transport, provider epoch, or product-policy authority.
document.objective: Consume one accepted LSLC-001N static-plus-description info tree and one accepted LSLC-001P volatile info tree into one target-bounded XmlElementTree whose direct children retain six static leaves, eleven volatile leaves, then desc in accepted LSLC-001H order, moving component allocations and remapping only the description subtree while adding no XML declaration, observed whitespace or self-closing spelling, provider, protocol, transport, runtime, device, feature, or Manifold authority behavior.
outputs: A dependency-free consuming merge of accepted N and P element trees into one info-rooted tree with exact static, volatile, then desc direct-child order.
Checked total-node and parent-index arithmetic, target-node rejection before one exact merged-arena reserve, and unchanged final XmlElementTree delegation.
Allocation-preserving movement of the static root/leaves, volatile leaves, desc root, and all description descendants with only the fixed eleven-node description offset applied.
Seven-case focused evidence for exact compact local element order and nested description preservation without promoting it to observed document spelling or endpoint compatibility.
